Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx basic,$0801,$ffff "AlternativeFormats\PRGs\Uncrunched\Cursed Key, The (Mad Productions - 2010).prg,0x0400,0x00EC" -Di_irq_exit=0 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 lda #$35 sta $01 jsr $c700 lda #$37 sta $01 jsr $c600 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Cursed Key, The (Mad Productions - 2010).prg"
Metadata.Notes:
Added a routine at $C600 to display the loading screen (the colour RAM is loaded for this to display correctly) and wait for any key or the fire button on either joystick to enter the game.
Note: as the screen memory for the loading screen at $C000 is partially overwritten during decompression when the game starts, it has been moved to $C800.
Also made the following changes to allow joystick button in port two to start the game: 1. Patched the JSR $AAD6 instructions at $ACFE and $BEE4 to be JSR $C860 instead 2. Added a small routine at $C860 which executes the routine at $AAD6 to carry on the normal code flow. It then checks the fire button in port 2 - if pressed, then it sets $C5 to $00 and returns, which causes the game to start just as if any key was pressed.
